title: Albanese announces new fuel security powers | ABC NEWS
description: Anthony Albanese has announced new fuel security powers to get "fuel restocked", so Australia is "ready for what may come". title: Exmouth picks up the pieces in wake of ex-Cyclone Narelle | ABC NEWS
description: Exmouth residents are assessing the damage by ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle after it hit the northern West Australian town as a category four system.  The town has been cut off by road, while Exmouth's airport runway has also been significantly damaged and the domestic terminal destroyed.   Reporting by Bridget McArthur. title: Houthis join Iran war, threatening more vital shipping lanes in the Red Sea | ABC NEWS
description: The Iranian-backed militant group, the Houthis, has announced it's joining the Iran war after firing a ballistic missile into Israel. title: St John Ambulance service fails overnight, 61 triple zero calls abandoned in NT | ABC NEWS
description: The St John ambulance service in the Northern Territory says it reached a critical failure point overnight, as it was forced to abandon more than 40 per cent of Triple Zero calls across Darwin and Palmerston.  The service says while no-one died as a result, for the first time, St John declared an Operational Capacity White which means it could not meet demands for the service, with a high chance of harm to patients or staff. Courtney Barrett-Peters has this report for ABC News Northern Territory.
